methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Kraken Robotics is a publicly listed company in the Defense & Security sector. The comparable universe consists of 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private companies, including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI. However, the peer set presents material constraints for valuation.

The Peer-Median triangulation method is not applicable to this analysis. The Defense & Security sector contains 21 same-sector peers overall, but filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comparables yields fewer than the three datapoints required for a reliable median derivation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce significant noise given Kraken's specialized position within Defense & Security and the structural differences between public and private peers at varying scales.

Without a sufficiently concentrated peer cohort meeting statistical thresholds, traditional multiple-based triangulation cannot be responsibly executed. The small public comp set (2 tickers) and the heterogeneous nature of the private peers—spanning different subsectors and capital structures—prevent confident synthesis into a single valuation range. Further work would require either identifying additional narrow-stage peers within Defense & Security, conducting revenue or EBITDA multiples analysis on the existing public tickers if financial data permits, or employing alternative methods such as discounted cash flow analysis if forward guidance is available.
